title: corydalis caseana subsp. caseana
intro: Corydalis caseana subsp. caseana, also known as the Himalayan Corydalis, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, particularly berberine. The medicinal properties of Corydalis caseana subsp. caseana have been found to provide relief from pain, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures and teas, and is traditionally consumed to aid in digestion and reduce stress.
